topic: 5 reasons African music gets rejected in sync
cta: Save this. Fix what you can. Then come find us.
caption_full:
It's not your sound. ↓

Here are the actual reasons African composers get passed over in sync licensing — none of them have anything to do with talent.

Save this. Fix what you can. Then come find us.

#SyncMaster #AfricanComposers #SyncLicensing #MusicBusiness #SyncReady

slides.body: Here are the actual reasons African composers get passed over in sync licensing — none of them have anything to do with talent. ↓

REASON 1: UNCLEARED SAMPLES
Even a small sample that isn't cleared makes the track legally untouchable.

REASON 2: SPLIT RIGHTS
Label owns your master. You own publishing.
Two separate negotiations for one placement.
Most supervisors have 20 other options. They move on.

REASON 3: NO METADATA
No BPM tag. No ISRC. No mood categorisation.

REASON 4: WRONG FILE FORMAT
MP3 files. SoundCloud links. Google Drive folders.
Supervisors need lossless files, properly named, ready immediately.

slides.headline: Open briefs. Every Tuesday.
slides.body: Composers: apply once, get matched for life. Supervisors: shortlist in 48h.
